aids.title: Kruskal = Edges First, Prim = Vertices First
aids.content: Kruskal's sorts EDGES globally and picks the smallest. Prim's grows from a VERTEX and expands. Remember: 'K for Kruskal = K for Kleinste (smallest) Edge globally. P for Prim = P for Point (vertex) expansion.'
aids.forRemembering: The fundamental difference between Kruskal's and Prim's approaches

aids.title: Kruskal's as Building Roads
aids.content: Imagine you have a list of all possible roads between cities sorted by cost. Kruskal's says: 'Build the cheapest road first, then the next cheapest, but skip any road that creates a loop (cycle).' You stop when all cities are connected.
aids.forRemembering: How Kruskal's algorithm works step by step

aids.title: Prim's as Growing a Tree
aids.content: Prim's algorithm is like growing a tree from a seed. You start at one city, then always extend to the nearest unconnected city. The tree grows outward one vertex at a time, always choosing the cheapest expansion.
aids.forRemembering: How Prim's algorithm works step by step

aids.title: Time Complexity Quick Reference
aids.content: Kruskal's: O(E log E) -- dominated by sorting edges. Prim's with heap: O(E log V) -- decrease-key operations. Prim's with matrix: O(V^2) -- scanning rows. For sparse graphs use Kruskal's. For dense graphs use Prim's with matrix.
aids.forRemembering: Time complexities of MST algorithms and when to use each
